title: Executive Order 9593—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Springfield Plywood Corporation, Located At Springfield, Oregon

flags.justification: Broad seizure and operating authority, meaningfully bounded by the protective requirements in Secs. 2 and 3, identical in structure to EO 9474/9476.
Sec. 5 sets a concrete 60-day cap after productive efficiency is restored.
Single purpose: seizure of this one company's plants.
Sec. 2 requires operation 'in accordance with the terms and conditions of employment which are in effect at the time possession thereof is taken.'
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
The 60-day cap in Sec. 5 is a genuine, explicit termination trigger.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
The 60-day cap and status-quo-employment-terms requirement provide real, checkable constraints.
A routine application of the established wartime plant-seizure template.
Sec. 3's explicit preservation of management's own functions and Sec. 4's cooperation directive are protective, anti-conflict design features.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/20 = 5.0%. First order in this corpus signed by President Truman rather than Roosevelt; structurally identical to the FDR-era EO 9474/9476 template, suggesting continuity in seizure-order design across the transition.
